title: Stay Balanced: - Acknowledge Both Sides: Write About Unpleasant Memories Alongside the Positive Ones
subtitle: Balance the Fading Affect Bias
description:
To stay balanced:
- Acknowledge both sides: Write about unpleasant memories alongside the positive ones.
- Reflect intentionally: Spend time thinking about what you learned from difficult moments.
- Focus on growth: Use past challenges as a reminder of your resilience.

Example: When recalling a failed project, consider the lessons learned rather than just forgetting the negatives.

howTo.title: Choose a memory to reflect on
howTo.summary: Select a specific memory, either positive or negative, that offers learning opportunities. Ensure it includes concrete details to enhance recall.

howTo.title: Write paired reflections
howTo.summary: Spend 10-20 minutes writing two paragraphs: one focusing on positive aspects and the other on negative elements and lessons learned.

howTo.title: Set a measurable micro-task
howTo.summary: Conclude your reflections with a specific, actionable lesson and a micro-task to implement within 48 hours.

howTo.title: Schedule regular practice
howTo.summary: Aim to complete this reflection exercise 3-5 times a week to build consistency and improve learning outcomes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your reflections and micro-task results in the Brali LifeOS app to track progress and insights. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/fading-affect-bias-journal-coach/.
